bergamot: Bright, zesty citrus like Earl Grey tea — fresh and slightly floral.
lemon: Sharp, clean citrus — like fresh lemon zest.
grapefruit: Tangy, slightly bitter citrus — sparkly and energetic.
orange: Sweet, juicy citrus — cheerful and round.
mandarin: Soft, sweet orange — gentle and friendly.
blood mandarin: Deep, sweet-tart orange with a richer edge.
blood orange: Juicy orange with a slightly tart, red-fruit twist.
neroli: Orange blossom flower — fresh, clean, slightly bitter-green and elegant.
orange blossom: White flower of the orange tree — sweet, clean, and soft.
pepper: Dry, peppery spice that adds a tingly kick.
black pepper: Sharp, dry, spicy — like fresh-ground pepper.
pink pepper: Light, rosy, sparkly pepper — fresh and a little fruity.
lavender: Clean, herbal, calming flower — barbershop classic.
ambroxan: Modern 'clean amber' — warm, slightly salty, skin-like and long-lasting.
ambrox: Same family as ambroxan — a warm, woody-amber skin scent.
ambrette: Musky seed with a soft, clean, slightly fruity-musk smell.
star anise: Sweet licorice spice — warm and aromatic.
vanilla: Sweet, warm, creamy — like vanilla cake batter.
cedar: Dry, pencil-shaving woods — clean and a bit sharp.
cedarwood: Dry, clean wood — like sharpened pencils.
sandalwood: Creamy, soft, milky wood — smooth and a little sweet.
mint: Cool, fresh, tingly — like toothpaste or fresh mint leaves.
ginger: Warm, zingy, slightly spicy root — fresh and lively.
jasmine: Rich, sweet white flower — heady and a little sensual.
nutmeg: Warm baking spice — cozy and slightly sweet.
incense: Smoky, churchy resin — meditative and a bit dry.
amber: Warm, sweet, resinous glow — cozy and a little powdery.
marine notes: Clean salty-water smell, like sea breeze.
aquatic: Fresh, watery, ozonic — like clean ocean air.
geranium: Green, rosy, slightly minty leaf — fresh and a little spicy.
sage: Dry, herbal, grey-green — aromatic and clean.
patchouli: Earthy, dark, slightly sweet — soil and old books vibe.
blackcurrant: Tart, dark berry — fruity with a slightly sharp edge.
green apple: Sharp, tart, very fresh green fruit.
musk: Soft, warm, clean skin-like smell — cozy and intimate.
oakmoss: Damp, earthy, forest-floor green — classic and a little bitter.
ambergris: Warm, salty, skin-like, slightly animalic — adds glow and depth.
cumin: Warm, slightly sweaty-spicy — adds a human, skin-like warmth.
leather: Smooth, dry, slightly smoky — like a new leather jacket.
tobacco: Sweet, dry, slightly smoky cured leaf — cozy and rich.
tobacco blossom: Soft, honeyed, floral side of tobacco.
coffee: Roasted, dark, slightly bitter-sweet — like fresh espresso.
iris: Powdery, elegant, slightly cool — makeup-and-violets vibe.
orris: Same as iris root — powdery, soft, expensive-smelling.
coal: Dry, mineral, slightly metallic smoky note.
cardamom: Aromatic green spice — fresh, slightly citrusy and warm.
rose: Classic flower — can be fresh, jammy, or spicy depending on blend.
spices: A warm mix of baking and kitchen spices.
cacao: Dry, dark chocolate — slightly bitter and cozy.
praline: Sweet caramelized nuts and sugar — dessert-like.
toffee: Buttery, caramelized sugar — gooey and sweet.
coconut wood: Creamy coconut with a smooth woody backbone.
chestnut: Warm, roasted, slightly sweet nutty smell.
guaiac wood: Smoky, smooth, slightly sweet tarry wood.
juniper: Dry, gin-like, fresh and slightly piney berry.
amberwood: Warm, dry, woody-amber — modern and radiant.
vetiver: Earthy, grassy, dry root — green and a little smoky.
tea: Soft, dry, slightly tannic and calming.
litchi: Juicy, sweet, slightly floral tropical fruit.
nesberry: Soft, sweet, slightly woody red berry.
tuberose: Big, creamy, heady white flower — rich and sensual.
gardenia: Creamy, lush, slightly green white flower.
hyacinth: Green, fresh, slightly sharp spring flower.
rum: Sweet, boozy, warm — like spiced dark rum.
vanilla orchid: Creamy vanilla with a soft floral edge.
flint: Cool, mineral, slightly smoky 'struck stone' smell.
spice: A warm hit of mixed spices.
oud: Rich, smoky, complex resinous wood — luxurious and intense.
cetalox: A clean ambroxan-type warm, woody-amber musk.
honey: Sweet, warm, slightly animalic golden note.
licorice: Sweet, dark, slightly medicinal anise — like black licorice candy.
mate: Dry, green, slightly smoky herbal tea.
thyme: Dry, herbal, slightly medicinal green herb.
suede: Soft, dry, gentle leather — smooth and powdery.
black cherry: Deep, sweet, slightly boozy dark cherry.
bitter almond: Sweet-nutty almond with a marzipan, slightly sharp edge.
agarwood: Same as oud — rich, smoky, resinous wood.
champagne: Sparkling, fizzy, slightly fruity celebratory note.
apricot: Soft, sweet, slightly tart stone fruit.
lychee: Juicy, sweet, slightly floral tropical fruit.
ginger lily: Soft, fresh, slightly spicy white flower.
orange flower: Soft, sweet, slightly honeyed white blossom.
oak: Dry, woody, slightly tannic barrel-wood note.
jasmine bud: Green, fresh, slightly sharp young jasmine.
african orange flower: Rich, honeyed, slightly green orange blossom.
pineapple leaf: Green, tart, slightly tropical leaf note.
turkish rose: Rich, deep, slightly jammy true rose.
spicy notes: A warm hit of mixed spices.
